title: Infrastructure, Risk & Resilience
overview: Module 5 brings the full assessment lifecycle together. You'll deploy a simulated client environment, discover and score vulnerabilities, build a risk matrix, and compile everything into a professional Engagement Packet — the same deliverable a real security consultant hands to a client.

units_breakdown.labDesc: Deploy LoneStar DevOps infrastructure, conduct vulnerability scanning, score top 10 findings with CVSS 3.1, build risk matrix and remediation roadmap.
Compile findings into a professional Engagement Packet. Executive summary for Amanda Nguyen (investors), technical runbook and remediation roadmap for Sarah Martinez.

clientContext.label: Client Context: LoneStar DevOps (Austin, TX)
clientContext.body: LoneStar is an 85-person SaaS startup with $12M ARR and a Series B on the horizon. Their infrastructure was set up by a developer who left six months ago. An enterprise prospect requires a third-party security assessment before signing. Amanda Nguyen (CEO) needs a report she can hand to Longhorn Ventures investors. Sarah Martinez (Senior DevOps) needs a roadmap she can actually execute with two engineers and no staging environment.

stakeholders.name: Amanda Nguyen
stakeholders.title: CEO & Co-Founder, LoneStar DevOps
stakeholders.concern: Longhorn Ventures wants a security report before they wire the Series B. I need confidence statements and timeframes I can actually defend in a board meeting — not a list of CVEs.

stakeholders.name: Sarah Martinez
stakeholders.title: Senior DevOps Engineer, LoneStar DevOps
stakeholders.concern: I’ve got two engineers, no staging environment, and a developer who left six months ago took half the institutional knowledge with him. Give me a remediation roadmap I can actually execute.

footer: Start with Lab 5.1 in the Assignments section.

notice.label: 🎯 Capstone Module:
notice.body: This is the complete engagement workflow in two labs: discover, score, prioritize, and report. You'll draw on skills from every previous module — reconnaissance methodology, network scanning, cryptographic concepts, and attack awareness — and apply them to a real risk management deliverable.
